title: Recovery vs. Jettison Ratio
query: SELECT COALESCE((SELECT SUM(Count) FROM CollectCargo WHERE MissionID IS NULL AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate), 0) AS collected, COALESCE((SELECT SUM(Count) FROM EjectCargo WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate), 0) AS ejected

details.text: {collected} unit(s) collected vs. {ejected} ejected during the report period. Net haul efficiency shows whether your salvage operations resulted in a net positive — a high jettison ratio relative to collection suggests indiscriminate scooping followed by painful hold-management decisions.
details.help: collected = free-scooped cargo; ejected = all EjectCargo events. collected drives the title as the recovery volume.

tagLines: Cargo recovery efficiency ratio compiled. Net haul volume vs. jettison loss calculated.
Some of what you scooped didn't make it to market. This is the gap between ambition and cargo capacity.
Efficient salvage means deciding what to keep before you scoop it. Experience teaches that lesson eventually.
